summary: Defines Classless Inter-domain Routing: prefix/length notation and the allocation and aggregation of the 32-bit IPv4 address space without classful boundaries. Specifies how an address block is characterized by a prefix and mask length, and the rules for hierarchical aggregation of routes. Best Current Practice (BCP 122); obsoletes RFC 1519. This is the authoritative reference for CIDR subnet arithmetic (network/broadcast derivation, prefix containment, block splitting).
title: Classless Inter-domain Routing (CIDR): The Internet Address Assignment and Aggregation Plan
